summary: Monster Foolish Heart, tight Box of Rain, soaring Playing In The Band
blurb: The show shines with a towering Foolish Heart and a crisp Box of Rain opener that fuels an explosive second set. Jerry’s lead work is on fire, Phil’s bass drives the band, and Brent adds lush textures throughout. A must‑listen for anyone tracking the Spring ’90 tour.
review: On March 19, 1990 at the Hartford Civic Center the Dead delivered one of the high points of their spring ’90 trek. The first set kicks off with a hard‑hitting "Hell In A Bucket" and rolls through classics before a standout moment arrives in the second set: a monster "Foolish Heart" where Jerry’s tone is described as perfect and his solo “monster‑level”. That energy cascades into a tight "Box of Rain", a soaring "Playing In The Band" that builds into an intense "Eyes Of The World" jam, and a beautifully space‑filled transition through drums and space into a moving "China Doll". Phil’s bass is right on the money throughout, especially on the ballads, while Brent’s keyboards add lush, tasteful fills that lift the whole performance. Both drummers lock in, with Bill’s heavy drive powering the fast jams and Mickey’s interplay adding color. The show flows superbly, making it a highlight of the tour and an essential listen for Deadheads.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All recorded analyses carry a positive sentiment, repeatedly praising the same songs (Foolish Heart, Box of Rain, Playing In The Band) and band members. Multiple sources note "monster" solos, "perfect" tone, and a "tight" second‑set flow, indicating strong consensus on show excellence.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard source with crystal‑clear mix, distinct instrument separation, and no dropouts; captures the full energy of the performance and highlights all standout moments.

key_highlights: Monster Foolish Heart with soaring lead work from Jerry
Tight Box of Rain opener that sets a high‑energy tone for set two
Explosive Playing In The Band/Eyes Of The World sequence leading into space
Rich, atmospheric China Doll and moving close through Brokedown Palace

band_performance.Jerry: Tone described as perfect; delivers a monster solo on Foolish Heart and soaring leads on Jack‑A‑Roe, with only minor lyric flubs noted.
band_performance.Phil: Leads the charge with monstrous, warm bass—right on throughout, especially prominent on Box of Rain, Foolish Heart, and China Doll.
band_performance.Bob: Provides solid rhythm and vocal contributions (notably on Jack‑A‑Roe); no major criticism recorded.
band_performance.Brent: Lush keyboard textures shine on Foolish Heart, Playin', and the second‑set jams; praised for tasteful fills and ambience.
band_performance.Mickey: Interplay noted in early drums of several songs; adds color to the drum/space sections.
band_performance.Billy: Hard, heavy drumming drives fast-paced Eyes jam; overall strong presence throughout the set.

must_listen_sequences: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad
